user_intent_preservation.original_goal: Migrate 3-phase PWM motor control from TC387 (GTM TOM + EVADC) to TC4D7 (eGTM ATOM + TMADC) on KIT_A3G_TC4D7_LITE.
user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate 3-phase, center-aligned, complementary PWM at 20 kHz with 1 µs dead-time on eGTM ATOM0 using channel pairs (U: CH0/1, V: CH2/4, W: CH6/8), use ATOM0.CH3 as phase-synchronous TMADC trigger via EGTM.ADC_OUT0; initialize duties to 25/50/75%; configure TMADC0 CH0–CH4 via IfxAdc_Tmadc, mapped to P40.0 (AN0) → CH0, P40.1 (AN1) → CH1, P40.2 (AN2) → CH2, P40.3 (AN3) → CH3, P40.4 (AN4) → CH4; configure pins via IfxEgtm_PinMap_setAtomTout and IfxPort; use IfxStm_get/IfxStm_getFrequency for 500 ms duty update cadence; initialize LED on P03.9 via IfxPort; map PWM to P20.8–P20.13; route trigger to P33.0; start PWM and ADC trigger synchronously; move duty updates to a 500 ms STM0 main-loop while polling latest TMADC results.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use unified IfxEgtm_Pwm to configure eGTM ATOM0 base + sync channels with DTM-managed 1 µs dead-time and center alignment; use IfxEgtm_Trigger_trigToAdc to route ATOM0.CH3 to EGTM.ADC_OUT0 for TMADC0 external triggering; configure TMADC0 CH0–CH4 via IfxAdc_Tmadc; configure pins via IfxEgtm_PinMap_setAtomTout and IfxPort; use IfxStm_get/IfxStm_getFrequency for 500 ms duty update cadence; initialize LED on P03.9 via IfxPort.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified eGTM PWM driver supports synchronized multi-channel, center-aligned, complementary PWM with DTM dead-time and is ideal for a 3-phase inverter on ATOM with synchronous start.

init_function.name: initEgtmAtom3phInv
init_function.signature: bool initEgtmAtom3phInv(EgtmAtom3phInvContext* ctx, const EgtmAtom3phInvConfig* cfg, volatile uint16* tmadcResultBuf, uint32 tmadcResultCount)
parameters: ctx: Module context; holds PWM/ADC handles and state (must not be NULL).
cfg: Static configuration (frequency=20 kHz, dead-time=1 µs, pin map, ATOM channel pairs, TMADC channel list).
tmadcResultBuf: External buffer for latest TMADC results (one entry per configured channel; written by polling).
tmadcResultCount: Number of TMADC channels configured (e.g., 5 for CH0–CH4); must match buffer length.
init_function.returns: true on successful peripheral configuration; false on parameter or driver init error

runtime_function.name: updateEgtmAtom3phInvDuty
runtime_function.purpose: Runtime duty update for three complementary phase pairs at 500 ms cadence in main loop while polling TMADC results.
runtime_function.signature: void updateEgtmAtom3phInvDuty(EgtmAtom3phInvContext* ctx, float dutyU_percent, float dutyV_percent, float dutyW_percent)
parameters: ctx: Module context initialized by initEgtmAtom3phInv.
dutyU_percent: Phase U duty in percent [0.0 .. 100.0].
dutyV_percent: Phase V duty in percent [0.0 .. 100.0].
dutyW_percent: Phase W duty in percent [0.0 .. 100.0].

start_function.name: startEgtmAtom3phInv
start_function.purpose: Atomically stage initial U/V/W duties (25%/50%/75%) and enable PWM outputs with trigger aligned to the PWM period so TMADC external trigger from ATOM0.CH3 is phase-synchronous.
parameters: ctx: Module context initialized by initEgtmAtom3phInv.

adc_results_interface.api: IfxAdc_Tmadc_getResult (or equivalent channel read API) invoked in the main loop
adc_results_interface.buffering: Latest-conversion values written into caller-provided tmadcResultBuf (passed to initEgtmAtom3phInv), indexed by TMADC channel ID
adc_results_interface.result_format: Right-aligned unsigned 16-bit per channel sample; application interprets scaling

migration_notes.context_preserved: The migration keeps the original control concept: 3-phase center-aligned complementary PWM using ATOM with 1 µs dead-time, synchronous ADC triggering from a dedicated ATOM channel, same PWM frequency and initial duties, specific pin routing (P20.8–P20.13 for PWM, P33.0 for trigger), LED GPIO, synchronous start, and 500 ms STM-based duty updates while polling TMADC results. All driver calls are mapped to TC4xx iLLD APIs available in the template.

testability.abstraction: Introduce lightweight driver interfaces (e.g., IEgtmPwm, IAdcTmadc) and pass them via EgtmAtom3phInvContext to enable dependency injection.
testability.mocking_strategy: Provide mock IEgtmPwm and IAdcTmadc implementations for CI that simulate timer progression and supply deterministic ADC samples from a software buffer.
testability.build_switch: Compile-time flag (e.g., UNIT_TEST) selects mock vs. iLLD-backed implementations.
